---
title: "Nakyung (Chloe) Oh"
---

415 W Gilman St, Apt. 508, Madison, WI 53703 | (608) 658-8035 | www.linkedin.com/in/nakyungoh

## Education

### Master of Science in Psychology: Data Science in Human Behavior

*University of Wisconsin-Madison, Madison, WI · Expected Graduation: December 2026*

### Bachelor of Science in Psychology, Minor in Statistics

*University of Cincinnati, Cincinnati, OH · Graduation: December 2024 · GPA 3.809*

## Projects & Research Experience

### Does Reading Matter

*Mentor: Dr. Nick Buttrick · March 2026 - Present*

- Investigate whether long-term reading habits influence worldview, thinking style, and psychological complexity
- Partner with team members to design AI prompts for coding and analyzing a Goodreads dataset
- Construct a custom GPT workflow integrated with the OpenAI API to automate qualitative coding and support large-scale text analysis

### Brain Emotional Regulation

*Mentor: Dr. João Guassi Moreira · March 2026 - May 2026*

- Applied decoding methods to build a neural signature that can identify emotion regulation states on unlabeled data
- Performed parcel-wise classification across 200 predefined brain regions using brain parcellation methods
- Built a custom K-nearest neighbors decoding model using an emotion regulation dataset, achieving the highest classification accuracy among the project group

### Reimplementation of Neural Network Model

*Instructor: Dr. Tim Rogers · January 2026 - May 2026*

- Reimplemented the Rumelhart and McClelland (1986) past-tense neural network model in Python using a modified verb dataset
- Tested whether the model could reproduce core behavioral patterns in language learning, including overregularization, recovery of irregular verbs, and U-shaped learning
- Evaluated model performance through accuracy analysis across training stages and verb categories

### Research Assistant

*Ulrich-Lai Laboratory - UC Metabolic Diseases Institute, Cincinnati, OH · May 2024 - December 2024*

- Analyzed the skills of monitoring and recording the rodents' social interaction behaviors using BORIS program
- Utilized the GraphPad PRISM program to analyze the statistical significance of rodents' behavior
- Conducted the ImageJ program on finding the intensity of the PNN and parvalbumin cells in the prefrontal cortex

### Data Collector

*'Play a Retro Video Game' Research - University of Cincinnati, OH · March 2024 - April 2024*

- Provided guidance to participants on playing the video game and offer assistance when needed
- Organized participants' registration timeslots and gather basic information
- Compiled data from participants surveys and gathered it into a single file for the researchers to use for analyzing

### Research Assistant

*Children Cognitive Research Lab - University of Cincinnati, Cincinnati, OH · September 2023 - August 2024*

- Developed proficiency in using Google Sheets for efficient data entry and organization
- Entered data collected from children at Corryville Elementary School into Google Sheets
- Collaborated with fellow lab members to score data and analyze children's cognitive math learning abilities

## Work Experience

### Academic Administrator

*Platinum SAT Academy, Seoul, South Korea · January 2025 - August 2025*

- Implemented a Google Sheets scheduling system to monitor student and teacher availability and improve class coordination
- Designed advanced vocabulary assessments to support students' language development for SAT exams
- Onboarded new administrative staff on operational procedures and effective use of Google Sheets management tools

### Pre-Art Therapy Intern

*University of Cincinnati Gardner Neuroscience Institute, Cincinnati, OH · January 2024 - May 2024*

- Conducted patient screenings for individuals with multiple sclerosis during art therapy sessions
- Prepared and organized essential supplies to facilitate therapy
- Engaged patients in reflective conversations about their completed artwork to support emotional expression and interpretation

## Skills

- **Language:** Korean (native), English (fluent)
- **Laboratory:** JASP, GraphPad PRISM, Behavioral Observation Research Interactive Software (BORIS), ImageJ
- **Software:** Microsoft Office (Word, PowerPoint, Excel), Google Sheets (Excel), R studio, SAS, Python
- **Certificate:** CITI program (ID: 12546421), Mental Health First Aid USA (ID: 220924681)
